steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Compute the third side using perimeter
steps.content:
The perimeter is 31 m and two sides are 7 m and 10 m. Let the third side be $x$. Then
$$x = 31 - 7 - 10 = 14$$
So the third side must be 14 meters.

steps.title: Verify triangle inequality and obtuse condition
steps.content:
Check triangle inequality:
$$7 + 10 \gt 14$$ (true)
$$7 + 14 \gt 10$$ (true)
$$10 + 14 \gt 7$$ (true)
All three hold, so a triangle exists.
To be obtuse, the square of the longest side must exceed the sum of the squares of the other two sides. The longest side is 14 m:
$$14^2 = 196$$
$$7^2 + 10^2 = 49 + 100 = 149$$
Since $$196 \gt 149$$, the triangle is obtuse.
Therefore the length 14 meters satisfies all conditions. That confirms D is correct.
